conbody.p: The Bi-directional Scalo 100 Autonomous Mobile Robot (AMR) is an automated system designed to transfer bumper components within a production line environment.

The AMR operates in both forward and reverse directions, enabling efficient material flow between workstations. It supports continuous movement of components and reduces dependency on manual handling.
The system includes a dedicated bumper fixture that securely holds components during transport. Operators interact with the system primarily for loading and unloading activities.
The AMR consists of integrated subsystems that support safe navigation, system control, and operator interaction. These include safety mechanisms, user interface components, and control elements.
The system is powered by an onboard rechargeable battery and uses a plug-in charging method for energy replenishment.
The AMR enhances operational efficiency by automating internal transport processes, improving workplace safety, and supporting consistent production workflows.
